reasons: Very low release-country accuracy (0.16) with reasoning indicating overly destructive country cleaning and many missing fused values; validation hint expects country output format as country names.

Explicit HIGH-severity diagnostic: list-format inconsistencies for genre (fused delimited string like "stage & screen|pop" vs gold scalar "Pop"), indicating inconsistent canonicalization of list-like encodings into the scalar style expected by validation.

HIGH-severity diagnostic: noisy list fusion / inflated lists for tracks_track_name/tracks_track_position/tracks_track_duration suggests list parsing/element normalization/deduplication is not consistent, which will break exact comparisons even when entities match.
No case-only mismatches detected, so normalization should focus on country canonicalization and list-like parsing rather than blanket lowercasing of value columns.
normalization_directives.recommendation_summary: Rerun normalization focused on (1) release-country canonicalization to country names without blanking multi-token values, and (2) robust list-like parsing/element normalization for track list fields (and delimiter handling for genre/label) while keeping value columns in validation’s scalar/casing style (no blanket lowercasing of name/artist/genre/label).

findings.title: Missing correspondence files for expected dataset pairs
Correspondence cluster structure suggests over-matching / over-clustering (non-long-tail, high one-to-many, or very large clusters)
List-format inconsistencies detected (scalar vs JSON-like lists vs delimited strings); may cause noisy list fusion and evaluation mismatches

columns.message: List-format inconsistency detected across artifacts (mix of scalar and list encodings).

recommendations.action: Generate correspondences for missing dataset pairs (run matcher for all dataset pairs).
Normalize list-like fields to a consistent representation (parse bracketed JSON-like lists, split delimiters, element-normalize, deduplicate, and choose fusion operator intentionally).

machine_hints.fusion_operator_hint: consider majority/intersection over union for noisy lists

Tighten matcher thresholds and/or add disambiguating rules/features to reduce over-matching (especially for pairs with high one-to-many).
For noisy list attributes, avoid union fusion; prefer majority vote, intersection, or source-priority with deduplication to reduce inflated lists.

=

[source]

Evaluation function using tokenized comparison with similarity threshold.
For lists: Uses Jaccard similarity between lists (order doesn’t matter).
For strings: Tokenizes and uses Jaccard similarity between token sets.
Useful for actor lists and titles where order and partial matches matter.
Parameters
:
fused_value
(
Any
) – The fused value to compare.
expected_value
(
Any
) – The expected value to compare against.
threshold
(
float
,
default 1.0
) – Minimum similarity threshold (0.0 to 1.0).
1.0 requires exact match, lower values allow partial matches.
Returns
:
True if similarity >= threshold, False otherwise.
Return type
:
bool
